authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Big Sur River within Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park
reaches.toDesc: Boundary of Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park within the Ventana Wilderness Area

reaches.name: Big Sur River within the Ventana Wilderness Area
reaches.fromDesc: Upstream end of the gorge pool at the boundary of Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park
reaches.toDesc: Within the Ventana Wilderness Area (upper regulated extent)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(11) — Big Sur River (Monterey Co.)
sources.quotedText: (11) Big Sur River (Monterey Co.). Big Sur river and tributaries above the upstream end of the gorge pool at the boundary of Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park within the Ventana Wilderness Area. Sat. preceding Memorial day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(17) — Big Sur River (Monterey Co.)
sources.quotedText: (17) Big Sur River (Monterey Co.). Big Sur River within Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park, east of the Highway 1 bridge, to its boundary within the Ventana Wilderness Area. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 269851, "Big Sur River", Stream, Monterey Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":269851,"gaz_name":"Big Sur River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Monterey"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13565389.6714, 4339289.9934) converts to lon -121.859969, lat 36.280524, at the river's mouth at the Pacific Ocean near Andrew Molera State Park, replacing the file's previous coordinate (-121.7590, 36.2410), which was off by roughly 10 km. Cross-checked against Wikipedia's infobox mouth coordinates (36°16'49.87"N 121°51'35.84"W = 36.2805194, -121.8599556), which match to within rounding.

parameters.note: Applies to Big Sur River and tributaries above the upstream end of the gorge pool at the boundary of Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park, within the Ventana Wilderness Area.

regulations.humanSummary: Open (catch-and-release only) Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30, on the Big Sur River and tributaries above the upstream end of the gorge pool at the Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park boundary, within the Ventana Wilderness Area
regulations.verbatimText: Big Sur river and tributaries above the upstream end of the gorge pool at the boundary of Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park within the Ventana Wilderness Area. Sat. preceding Memorial day through Sep. 30.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing all year within Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park, from the Highway 1 bridge (east side) to the park's boundary within the Ventana Wilderness Area — the segment immediately downstream of the open, catch-and-release-only reach above the gorge pool at that same boundary.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, within Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park from the Highway 1 bridge to the park's boundary within the Ventana Wilderness Area
regulations.verbatimText: Big Sur River within Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park, east of the Highway 1 bridge, to its boundary within the Ventana Wilderness Area. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
